message:
fix(spawn): suppress fresh-project recovery-inbox warning (#108)

On a brand-new project that has no registered agents yet, `ntm spawn`
was printing an alarming

  ⚠ Recovery context: agent mail: fetch inbox: agentmail:
    fetch_inbox failed: tool error: Agent '<session>' not found.
    Project '<path>' has no registered agents yet. ...

line for every fresh spawn, even though the spawn itself proceeded
cleanly ("✓ Recovery context prepared for session" immediately
afterwards). The warning is the expected, benign result of probing
an inbox that does not yet exist — there is no prior session to
recover from on first run — so surfacing it as a warning was pure
noise that masked real recovery failures behind a false signal.

The fix is in `fetchRecoveryInbox` (internal/cli/spawn.go). After
iterating the candidate agent names, if the final error is one of:

  - agentmail.ErrAgentNotRegistered
  - agentmail.ErrNotFound (how `agentmail.mapJSONRPCError` maps
    "Agent '...' not found" JSON-RPC responses from the server)
  - or a plain-text error whose message contains both "agent" and
    "not found", or "has no registered agents" (belt-and-suspenders
    for wrappers that eat the sentinel)

we treat the result as an empty inbox and return `(nil, "", nil)`.
Real fetch failures — transport errors, auth errors, server unavailable,
any non-"agent not found" error — still propagate unchanged and still
surface as warnings.

Tests (internal/cli/spawn_recovery_test.go):

- TestFetchRecoveryInbox_FreshProjectAgentNotFoundIsNotAnError:
  an ErrNotFound-wrapped "Agent '<session>' not found" response
  resolves to `(nil, "", nil)`.
- TestFetchRecoveryInbox_RealFailuresStillPropagate: an
  ErrServerUnavailable response still surfaces as a wrapped error
  (preventing the filter from swallowing real failures).
- TestIsRecoveryEmptyInboxError: direct table test of the
  typed-error sentinels and the fallback string heuristics.

Closes #108.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
